steps.title: Recognize the y-intercept from the given point
steps.content: The point (0, 60) has x-coordinate 0, so it lies on the y-axis. Any point on the y-axis gives us the y-intercept. Therefore, $b = 60$.

steps.title: Write the equation using slope-intercept form
steps.content:
With $m = -8$ and $b = 60$, substitute into the slope-intercept form to get:
$$y = -8x + 60$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
